payload.text: a app that go paste context either plain text or link then through chat window and capture visible contexts or with ability scroll up to revealed entire chat (optional), collected chat and summarize the contents of the provide document or chat with exclude option to filter code, and custom specific content

**Defining App Requirements**

I'm currently focused on defining the core requirements for this React application. Specifically, I'm solidifying the need for text/link input, chat context handling, and a chat window for interaction. The summarization functionality and code block filtering are also top of mind, shaping the initial scope of the project.


**Refining Core Functionality**

I'm now honing in on key features. The focus is now on user input: text/link areas, and processing with Gemini. I'm solidifying the "Exclude Code" and "Focus on Keywords" toggle options. A chat interface is in the plan, allowing user questions about the analyzed content and a summary dashboard for clarity. Component design involves `InputArea`, `ChatWindow`, `SummaryPanel`, and `ControlPanel`. I plan to use a `geminiService.ts` for API calls.


**Detailing Technical Specs**

I'm now diving into the technical specifics. I've defined global types in `types.ts` and am planning a professional, dev-focused theme. The layout will likely be split-screen or sidebar-based for input/settings and the main area. Accessibility, with good contrast and clear buttons, is a priority. I'm focusing on user input, and will interpret the user requirement of scrollable context as a way to paste and intelligently handle large documents. I've decided to use the `gemini-3-flash-preview` model and will use system instructions for the "Exclude Code" logic. I have also decided that my state management will need `rawContext`, `filteredContext`, `messages`, `summary`. The filtering logic is to be done through Gemini, since code-block filtering is more complicated than can be done through regex.

Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and FontAwesome.

Main application component managing state and layout.

payload.text: Add an option to export the generated summary to a text file.

**Adding Export Functionality**

I'm currently focused on implementing an "Export Summary" function. I've decided to add a button to the UI to trigger this feature. My immediate next step is creating a download function to generate a text file containing the summary text, and then incorporating the new button into `App.tsx`.

entries.description: Added export summary functionality and a button to trigger it in the summary view.

payload.text: Add a loading state to the chat input. When the user sends a message, disable the input and show a spinner or 'processing' indicator until the AI responds.

**Initiating Loading State**

I'm working on implementing a loading state for the chat input. My current plan involves adding a boolean state variable, let's call it `isChatting`, to track the waiting period after a message is sent. The goal is to set this to `true` when `handleSendMessage` is called and back to `false` when a response is received, providing visual feedback to the user in `App.tsx`.


**Refining State Management**

I'm now revisiting state management, realizing `isProcessing` is occupied. I'll add a separate `isChatting` state to `App` for loading state tracking, avoiding conflicts with the "Summarize" function. I'll update `handleSendMessage` to manage this new state effectively, disabling the input and showing a loading indicator while the request is in progress.


**Implementing Chat Logic**

I've integrated the `isChatting` state variable into `handleSendMessage`. I'm now setting `isChatting` to `true` at the beginning of the function and back to `false` in the `try` block after receiving a response from the service. Also, I've added a check to ensure no new messages are sent if one is already in transit. Error handling now sets the new state to 'false' even if there is an error.

entries.description: Added a loading state (isChatting) to handle and visualize message processing in the chat interface.

payload.text: Integrate a file upload component that allows users to upload documents (e.g., .txt, .pdf) to be analyzed, in addition to pasting text.

**Considering File Uploads**

I'm currently focused on integrating a file upload feature into `ContextWhisper`. I'm thinking about supporting `.txt` and `.pdf` formats specifically. The primary goal is to extract the content from these files and load it into the `rawContext` area to facilitate summarization and chat, but I may consider processing it directly.


**Implementing File Handling**

I've made progress on file handling. I've modified `App.tsx` to include file input. Now I'm focused on the logic for reading `.txt` files, successfully using `FileReader.readAsText`. For PDFs, I'm considering several options: a robust `FileReader` with a fallback message, or CDN inclusion of `pdfjs-dist` to deliver a world-class experience. The sidebar UI will include an upload area. The behavior will be: upload/drag, then text appended or a PDF prompt.


**Refining UI & Flow**

I'm now zeroing in on the user interface and overall flow for file uploads. The current focus is on a streamlined and intuitive experience. I've decided to implement a stylized "Upload" icon button near the "Paste Content" label to trigger the file input. Text files will be read directly into the text area. I'm exploring a standard CDN version of `pdfjs-dist` to deliver good PDF parsing, and will focus on keeping it light. Upon file selection, the file's content will immediately populate the textarea.

entries.description: Added PDF.js library to support document parsing for file uploads.
Integrated file upload functionality for .txt and .pdf files, including text extraction logic and UI components.

payload.text: Enhance the error handling for chat messages. If an error occurs during sendMessage, display a clear error message to the user and provide a retry option.

**Improving Error Handling**

I'm working to enhance the error handling for chat messages. My current focus is refining the `sendMessage` function to provide a better user experience when failures occur. Currently, it just displays an error message, but now I'm aiming for something better. I'm focusing on displaying a clearer error and providing an easy retry option. The goal is to make the user experience much smoother in the face of temporary issues. I'm starting by modifying the `App.tsx` file.


**Refining Error States**

I'm now zeroing in on integrating the error states within the existing chat structure. I've updated the `Message` interface to include an optional `isError` flag. In `App.tsx`, I'm working to integrate a retry button directly into the chat flow when `isError` is true. The aim is to create a seamless experience where the user can easily re-attempt sending a failed message without leaving the chat interface. I'm focusing on ensuring the retry mechanism targets the last attempted message.

entries.description: Added isError optional property to the Message interface to identify failed AI responses.
Enhanced chat error handling by adding a retry mechanism and specific styling for error messages in the chat interface.

payload.text: In the chat tab, implement functionality to display the history of messages between the user and the AI, preserving the conversation context. Each message should include its role (user/model), content, and timestamp. Display the most recent messages at the bottom.

**Developing Chat Tab**

I'm focusing on enhancing the chat tab within `ContextWhisper`. My goal is to display the complete message history between the user and the AI. Thankfully, preserving the context is already taken care of by existing functions. Now I am building the framework for the tab.


**Refining Message Display**

I'm refining how messages are displayed in the chat tab. I'm ensuring each message includes the role, content, and timestamp, with the latest at the bottom. The existing structure in `App.tsx` is already largely in place. `messages` state holds the data, and there's a good foundation for role-based styling and a scroll-to-bottom function triggered by message updates.


**Assessing Current Functionality**

I'm realizing the chat tab's core functionality may already be implemented, specifically within `App.tsx`. The existing code handles displaying message history with roles, content, and timestamps, and uses `scrollToBottom`. It seems the Gemini service also keeps the chat context through `ai.chats.create`. The prompt may be satisfied already. I'm exploring potential gaps, such as a "Clear Chat" feature, or refining the existing layout further.


**Analyzing Message Rendering**

I'm now diving deeper into how messages render within the chat interface. I'm focusing on the details of the message component. I see the importance of visually distinguishing user and AI messages with different background colors and rounded corners. I'm also noticing the use of timestamps and error states, along with a retry button for errors. I think that the current implementation is very effective for user interaction.


**Improving UI/UX**

I'm now focused on refining the user interface and user experience of the chat tab. It seems a "Clear Chat" feature would be a valuable addition, as users might expect to manage their chat history. I'm also considering some minor visual enhancements to make the interface feel more professional. The existing structure and message display seem solid; now it's about polishing the finer details and adding user controls. Finally, I'm checking that the history synchronization between the UI and Gemini's chat instances is handled correctly.


**Clarifying Requirements**

I'm reassessing the prompt's intent. The user may be checking on recent progress. I'm focusing on solidifying current functionality, and adding features to enhance the chat experience. I'm adding a "Clear Chat" button, a scroll-to-bottom button and improving the visual distinctions between message types. I am updating `App.tsx` accordingly. I will ensure clear message bubbles and a bit more polish.

entries.description: Enhanced the chat tab by adding a "Clear History" button, improving message layout, and ensuring a polished conversation experience with role-based styling and timestamps.

payload.text: Implement undo/redo functionality for the text editing of the raw context. Allow users to revert or reapply changes made in the textarea. language option {english. thai} for now

**Implementing New Features**

I've been working on integrating undo/redo functionality for the `rawContext` textarea. I'm also adding a language selection feature with English and Thai as the initial options. I'm focusing on state management for both features and then will create the UI elements. Finally, the UI will be updated to display them.


**Refining Specifications Further**

I'm now refining the Undo/Redo logic. I've decided to use two stacks, `past` and `future`, for the text area. Every change pushes the current text to the `past` stack. `undo` will pop from `past` and push the current state to the `future` stack.

entries.description: Added language property to AnalysisState and updated Message to include language context.
Updated GeminiService to support multi-language summaries and chat responses based on user selection.
Implemented Undo/Redo for the context textarea and added Thai/English localization support for the entire interface.

payload.text: Implement functionality to save the chat history to localStorage so it persists across browser sessions.

**Considering Local Storage**

I'm currently focused on persisting chat history with `localStorage`. My plan involves two key steps: saving messages whenever they're updated and loading them upon app initialization. This is straightforward, but I need to consider storage limits and potential data structure optimizations for this implementation.


**Implementing Local Storage**

I've made progress on local storage implementation. I'm focusing on saving messages and clearing them when the user selects "clear history," adjusting `App.tsx` and the `handleClearHistory` function. Serialization and deserialization of `Date` objects are now handled. I'm prioritizing the message persistence to align with the prompt, but I see the value in also storing the raw context and analysis state to fully recreate the experience. I'll stick to saving messages for now.


**Expanding Persistence Strategies**

I'm now expanding my persistence strategy. I'll save both `messages` and the broader `state` (raw context, summary, etc.) to `localStorage`. Upon mounting the app, I'll load both. If a `summary` exists after loading, I'll initialize the chat via `geminiService.initChat`. The keys for storage are `cw_state` and `cw_messages`. I'm mindful of parsing `Date` objects. I need to handle potential initial state overwrites during saving.

entries.description: Implemented persistent storage for chat history and analysis state using localStorage, ensuring the conversation and context remain available across browser sessions.

payload.text: When the chat tab is active and there are messages, add a button to generate a summary of the current chat conversation. This summary should be displayed in a dedicated section or modal.

**Considering Chat Summary Button**

I'm currently focused on the feasibility of adding a summary generation button directly within the chat interface. My thoughts revolve around ensuring it's only visible when relevant chat messages exist. The goal is to provide a clean and accessible summary feature in a new section or modal.


**Implementing Chat Summary Feature**

I'm now diving into the implementation details for summarizing chat histories. I'm focusing on the `geminiService` update, specifically the `summarizeChat` method, which will handle the chat history summary. I'm also planning the necessary UI adjustments. This includes adding a state variable for the chat summary and a new state for the summary loading state. Finally, I'm thinking of adding a new button or a toggleable section in the chat header, ensuring it only appears when there are messages to summarize.

entries.description: Added summarizeChat method to allow generating summaries of the ongoing conversation history.
Added "Summarize Chat" functionality with a modal display, updated translations, and enhanced the Chat tab UI.

payload.text: Save and load the chat history using localStorage, so conversations persist across browser sessions.

**Implementing Local Storage**

I'm focusing on integrating local storage to save and load chat history, so sessions persist. I've found existing logic in `App.tsx` that initializes `messages` from `localStorage`, which is a good starting point. I am now evaluating the full implementation.


**Verifying Data Persistence**

Okay, after a closer look, the message loading and saving using local storage appears to be fully operational within the provided code. The useEffect hook ensures data persistence. I have verified that messages are being loaded from, and saved to localStorage using the key 'cw_persisted_messages'. It seems this aspect is already addressed within the current application's implementation.


**Analyzing Chat History Integration**

I'm now digging deeper into how chat history interacts with the GeminiService, particularly after reloads. The `geminiService.initChat` function appears to initialize a *new* chat instance, meaning any persistent chat history must be explicitly managed elsewhere. The existing `useEffect` in `App.tsx` initializes `geminiService` when `state.summary` and `state.rawContext` are present. This means a new chat starts upon reloading.


**Enhancing Chat Initialization**

I've realized that while the UI displays the chat history from local storage, the Gemini AI instance isn't aware of it during a new session. Specifically, `initChat` in `GeminiService` creates a *new* chat without the previous history, leading to context loss. I'm focusing on modifying `initChat` to receive and utilize the `messages` array for context persistence, using the `history` parameter available within the `@google/genai` library's `chats.create` method to fully support persistent conversations.


**Refining History Integration**

I'm now implementing the history integration with the Gemini service. The plan is to update `GeminiService` to use the `history` parameter during chat initialization, map the local `Message[]` data structure to the `@google/genai` library's `Content[]` format, and pass this mapped history to `initChat`. I'm also considering the potential for redundant history when a system instruction is present.


**Updating Gemini Service**

I'm now modifying `GeminiService.ts` to integrate chat history. I've added a `history` parameter to `initChat`, and I'm mapping the roles between my app and Gemini's expected formats ('model' to 'model', 'user' to 'user'). Also, I have updated `App.tsx` to pass the `messages` array to `geminiService.initChat`, ensuring context persistence across sessions. Now I'm reviewing types and imports.


**Refining Error Handling**

I'm now refining the history integration in `GeminiService` to exclude messages flagged as errors from the history sent to Gemini. My `Message` interface provides the `isError` flag for this purpose. I am updating `GeminiService` to filter the history based on this flag before sending the mapped messages to the Gemini API. In `App.tsx`, I will update chat initialization and summary generation to properly utilize the updated `GeminiService` history. I'm carefully reviewing the type definitions.


**Confirming Implementation Details**

I've confirmed that `App.tsx` *already* handles `localStorage` logic for displaying history, but not for providing it to Gemini. I will now integrate this by passing the message history to `initChat` in `GeminiService` to create a "truly" persistent AI memory. I must also save `messages` whenever it is updated, as well as ensure the history passed to Gemini is filtered for error messages.


**Evaluating Persistence Strategies**

I'm now assessing how to persist other aspects of the application state beyond chat history. The `state` and `messages` are already managed. I realize `chatSummary` and `showChatSummaryModal` should also be persisted across sessions, unlike past/future states. I need to figure out the best approach for saving and loading these elements to maintain a consistent user experience. I am now evaluating implementation.


**Refining Data Persistence**

I'm now implementing logic to persist additional state elements besides the chat history. I am currently considering a mechanism to persist and restore `chatSummary` and `showChatSummaryModal` across user sessions. I need to ensure that these elements are appropriately saved and loaded from local storage to maintain a consistent user experience and state. I am checking storage limitations as well.

To ensure a truly persistent experience where the AI also remembers the previous conversation after a page reload, I have updated the `GeminiService` to accept and initialize with history. I've also refined the `App` component to seamlessly pass the stored messages into the chat session upon initialization.

### Technical Specification:

1.  **AI History Synchronization**: Modified `GeminiService.initChat` to accept an array of `Message` objects. It now maps these internal message objects (filtering out errors) to the official Gemini `Content` format (`role` and `parts`) and passes them to `ai.chats.create`. This ensures the model maintains context continuity across browser sessions.
2.  **State Rehydration**: Updated the `useEffect` hook that runs on application mount to include the `messages` loaded from `localStorage` when re-initializing the Gemini chat instance.
3.  **UI Consistency**: Reinforced the `localStorage` persistence logic for both the analysis state (raw context, summary, settings) and the message history.

entries.description: Updated initChat to support passing message history to the Gemini API, ensuring the AI model maintains context after page reloads.
Updated chat initialization to include the persisted history from localStorage when the app loads.

payload.text: Implement a history feature for document summaries, allowing users to view and revert to previous summaries of the same document.

**Envisioning History for Summaries**

I'm focusing on enabling users to view and revert to prior summaries of a document. It seems like the existing `rawContext` is a useful starting point for tracking different iterations. I'm considering what triggers will activate storage of past summaries and also the specific data points to record in the history feature.


**Implementing Summary History**

I've decided to save every generated summary to the history. This will give users maximum flexibility in viewing their progression. I'll add a new state variable, `summaryHistory`, to store an array of summary objects, including the ID, timestamp, summary text, focus keywords, and code exclusion setting. I'm leaning toward a "History" button that opens a modal for browsing the past summaries, similar to the chat summary modal.


**Refining History Implementation**

I'm updating `types.ts` and `AnalysisState` to include `SummaryHistoryItem` and `summaryHistory`, and I'm updating `App.tsx` to save new summaries to history. The UI is the next big step, and I'm exploring options like a sidebar or a summary tab. I like the idea of a "clock" icon to trigger a modal. It's crucial that users can see the history of a document summary, even if the document text is slightly altered. I'll add the new one to the history too so it is complete. I'll add "Summary History" and "Restore" for the history UI.


**Finalizing History Features**

I'm now integrating the history feature. I've defined `SummaryHistoryItem` in `types.ts`, and I'm initializing and updating `summaryHistory` in `App.tsx` using `localStorage`. The History button in the Summary tab is implemented with the `SummaryHistoryModal`. I'm using `rawContext` to treat "same document" updates and store new summaries, refining the "Restore" function to update current summary, keywords, and exclude code settings. I have updated `types.ts`, and `translations` are added.

entries.description: Added SummaryHistoryItem interface and updated AnalysisState to include history tracking.
Implemented Summary History feature allowing users to view, browse, and restore previous document summaries. Added modal UI for history management and localized strings.

payload.text: Integrate a file upload component that allows users to upload documents (e.g., .txt, .pdf) to be analyzed, in addition to pasting text.

**Integrating File Upload**

Okay, I'm focusing on integrating a file upload component into the existing application. I'm reviewing the existing file upload logic, specifically `handleFileUpload` and `extractTextFromPdf`, to ensure the new component seamlessly complements it. The main goal is to create a visible and well-integrated experience for the user.


**Refining the Upload UX**

I'm now zeroing in on UI enhancements to the file upload experience. The application already has a functional file upload via button, but I'm thinking of adding a drag-and-drop zone, possibly overlaying the existing text area or as a distinct section, which would make the entire process much smoother for the user. I've been reviewing the existing code and CSS, and I'm currently assessing where to add the new "drop zone" component.


**Enhancing the Upload Interface**

I am now focusing on implementing a dedicated drag-and-drop zone within the application. I've decided to integrate this zone with the existing text area, adding a visual indicator like "Drop files here" in the sidebar when the area is empty. I plan to enhance `App.tsx` by including `onDragOver`, `onDragLeave`, and `onDrop` handlers to facilitate this interaction. This refined approach provides a more intuitive way for users to upload files, especially complementing the text-pasting functionality.

To enhance the user experience and fulfill the request for a more robust file upload integration, I will implement a dedicated **Drag & Drop Zone** within the sidebar. This component will provide a highly intuitive way to ingest documents, complementing the existing "Paste" functionality.

### Specification:

1.  **Visual Drop Zone**: A dedicated dashed-border area in the sidebar that reacts to drag-and-drop events. It will feature an icon and clear instructions, utilizing the `file-drop-zone` styles defined in the HTML.
2.  **Stateful Feedback**: The zone will visually change (highlight/glow) when a file is hovered over it, providing immediate affordance to the user.
3.  **Seamless Integration**: Dropped files (PDF or TXT) will be processed automatically using the existing extraction logic, and their content will populate the context area.
4.  **UX Polish**: Added a "Reading file..." loading state specifically for the drop zone to ensure the user knows processing is happening.

entries.description: Implemented a dedicated Drag & Drop file upload component in the sidebar with visual feedback and integrated it with existing PDF/TXT extraction logic.

payload.text: Implement a search bar within the chat tab that allows users to search through their conversation history for specific keywords or phrases. also add setting option page for adjust model temperature and limite token output and allow to use different api, custom context and persona to work on the project with correct tension.

**Developing New Features**

I'm currently focused on incorporating a search bar directly into the chat tab of the "ContextWhisper" app. This feature aims to enable users to efficiently sift through their conversation history. Furthermore, I've begun planning an options page, likely as a modal or new view, where users can tweak settings like model temperature, max output tokens, and define custom persona/system instructions to tailor the app's behavior.

entries.description: Updated AnalysisState to include model settings (temperature, tokens, system instruction) and history tracking.
Updated GeminiService to support dynamic model configuration including temperature, max tokens, and custom system instructions.
Integrated Chat Search and Settings configuration page. Added UI for adjusting temperature, token limits, and custom persona instructions.
